programmes.show_name: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programmes.show_description: We look at the rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn - footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences.

programmes.show_name: Stacey Dooley: Face to Face with Armageddon
programmes.show_description: The journalist meets Americans prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest.
Alex starts her training as a fertility assistant at King's Fertility, London, and is put straight to work caring for patients going through IVF treatment.
During her second week at King's Fertility, Alex cares for patients going in and out of theatre and witnesses the emotional turmoil and resilience of the patients undergoing IVF.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including stained glass and a Victorian hay rack that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners.
Torquay: In Torquay, the team help multiple sclerosis sufferer Stuart, his daughter Lauren and his mother Lin, who cares for Stuart despite being completely blind.
Plymouth: In Plymouth, the team help the Morfey family. Two of the boys have cerebral palsy, and the family has been struggling to make their multi-level home disabled-friendly.

Trigger, Fury & Raven: Police dog Trigger has crushed sciatic nerves, feisty kitten Fury has a tibia injury, and Shepadoodle Raven has double hip dysplasia.
Sunny, Bertie & Edith: Bouncy Golden Retriever puppy Bertie has a dislocated hip, Tibetan terrier Sunny is almost immobile, and Springer Spaniel Edith has a bulging spinal disc.
Hessle: The DIY SOS team and hundreds of kind volunteers step up to help the recently widowed and wheelchair-bound Darren and his three sons.
Torquay: In Torquay, the team help multiple sclerosis sufferer Stuart, his daughter Lauren and his mother Lin, who cares for Stuart despite being completely blind.
The paramedics treat a young mum with severe stomach pains, take a breathless pensioner to hospital and attend an elderly lady with dementia whose nose will not stop bleeding.
Hit the road with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. This episode features a stand-off involving a man with a samurai sword in South Yorkshire.
The fast-paced reality series continues. HART staff must treat one of their own after a freak accident involving a 4x4 leaves an off-duty medic with serious injuries.

programmes.show_name: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programmes.show_description: Trevor McDonald visits US jails holding notorious female criminals - Indiana Women's Prison and Rockville Correctional Facility.

programmes.show_name: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programmes.show_description: Second in a two-part documentary. Trevor meets a woman who killed her baby son, one who escaped and was on the run for 35 years, and another woman who will never be released.
Alex is now one month into her training as a fertility assistant and she is finally able to work independently on the early pregnancy scan list.
Alex spends her first shift in Andrology learning about how they analyse and store sperm. Later, she works with a patient whose only hope of having children is by using donor eggs.

Too Hot to Handle: Part 2: Stuart finally catches up with Stevie and Rob, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape.
Too Hot to Handle: Part 3: Stevie reveals to Stuart why she rescued Towler and makes plans to flee the country, but the violent gang pursuing them seems to be two steps ahead.
Second Chance: Mel's younger brother and his girlfriend come to stay for New Year's Eve and she believes they are out happily celebrating - until there is a stabbing at a nightclub.
The Morning After: When a young mother is found badly beaten in an alleyway, Sun Hill CID is drawn into her tale of debt, blackmail and a complex love life.
Little Girl Lost: Part 1: A couple return from a night away to find their baby has been abducted from her bed, and the story offered by the child's nanny turns out to be unreliable.

Reality series following the work of Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. Paramedic Rochers fights to save a DIY mechanic crushed by his own car.
Reality series following the work of Yorkshire Hazardous Area Response Team. The team try to seal a major chemical spill and help a teenager who fell down a chimney.
Reality series following Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. In this edition the team race to save a man who has been shot outside a mosque.
More real-life drama with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. The medics are called to support a bomb squad, and help a teenager impaled on a gate.
Matron Steph and James treat some very sick patients on the busiest shift Steph has seen in the emergency dept, while Caitlin cares for a good Samaritan in a lot of pain.
It's Halloween and Matron Hazera is having a staffing nightmare, Matron Steph meets an abusive patient in police custody, and Amy helps deliver premature identical twins via c-section.
Popular sketch series co-written and performed by the versatile comedy actress. In this episode, 'Gingers for Justice' take to the rooftops! With special guest Patsy Palmer.
More award-winning comedy co-written and performed by the versatile actress. Sketch highlights include Nan's unlikely appearance on The Paul O'Grady Show.
More amusing archetypes and infectious catchphrases in the award-winning sketch show. Nan's sister comes over from Spain, Derek gets a massage and Lauren gets hitched.
